mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative risk sentiment and downward S&P 500 price pressure over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in >70% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost inflation pressures and supports positive equity returns during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in >65% of observations during easing cycles, or the predictive IC drops below zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with stronger corporate profit fundamentals, generating immediate positive market repricing and upward S&P 500 momentum over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 revisions fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns, with a hit rate below 50% and a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index above 270.0 indicates entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >70% of periods when CPIAUCSL remains above 270.0, or the signal out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 20-day returns in >60% of triggered months, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during a high-inflation environment signals eroding real consumer purchasing power, triggering immediate profit downgrades and negative S&P 500 price action over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative retail sales growth coincides with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in >65% of cases during high-inflation periods, or the predictive accuracy fails to exceed a buy-and-hold benchmark.
